channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Critique] SAP warns of critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
item.description: SAP has addressed 16 vulnerabilities across multiple products as part of its July 2026 security updates, including three critical flaws in NetWeaver, Commerce Cloud, and AppRouter. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft starts testing cleaner Windows Search without ads
item.description: Microsoft is now testing a cleaner and faster version of Windows Search that should prioritize relevant results over ads and promotional content. [...]

item.title: [Critique] US sanctions VPN, malware providers for enabling ransomware attacks
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned two individuals and one entity for enabling ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Grok Build Uploaded Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Read
item.description:
xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed.
A researcher publishing as cereblab,…

item.title: [Critique] U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including…

item.title: [Élevé] 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
item.description:
A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog.
The…

item.title: [Critique] Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
item.description:
Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform.
The way in…

item.title: [Faible] Hackers steal Lidl customer data from external service provider
item.description: The retailer said the incident did not affect its online shopping platform itself but involved a separately stored customer database maintained by a third-party provider. According to notifications sent to Lidl's…

item.title: [Faible] EU leaders eye social media ban for children under age 13
item.description: “While ultimately it is up to parents to decide when children get their first smartphones, what we already have is a consensus that there needs to be a start date for the age children can join social media,” says…

item.title: [Élevé] Japan's largest taxi operator shuts systems after cyberattack
item.description: Japan's largest taxi operator, Nihon Kotsu, announced that its systems were compromised in a cyberattack, forcing the company to shut down part of its infrastructure. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
item.description: The Jscrambler client-side web security company disclosed that a threat actor published a malicious version of its npm package that has been downloaded almost 1,500 times. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
item.description: A new macOS information-stealing malware called CrashStealer pretends to be Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal credentials, keychain data, and crypto wallets. [...]

item.title: [Critique] VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."

item.title: [Élevé] France Pare-Brise : 100 000 dossiers avec permis, cartes grises et IBAN revendiqués
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à France Pare-Brise a été revendiquée en juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir exploité... L’article France Pare-Brise : 100 000 dossiers avec permis, cartes grises et IBAN revendiqués est…

item.title: [Élevé] e-Benefits Prévoyance : 6 420 utilisateurs en fuite après une faille IDOR
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à e-Benefits Prévoyance a été revendiquée le 12 juillet 2026. Les auteurs affirment... L’article e-Benefits Prévoyance : 6 420 utilisateurs en fuite après une faille IDOR est apparu en…

item.title: [Élevé] Betrail : 2,8 millions de profils de coureurs dans une fuite massive
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Betrail.run a été revendiquée le 13 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme diffuser une... L’article Betrail : 2,8 millions de profils de coureurs dans une fuite massive est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Élevé] KeepCool : 287 842 fiches d’adhérents en fuite après une faille
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à KeepCool a été revendiquée sur un forum cybercriminel le 11 juillet 2026.... L’article KeepCool : 287 842 fiches d’adhérents en fuite après une faille est apparu en premier sur…

item.title: [Élevé] EIML Paris : près de 90 000 personnes exposées après la cyberattaque du Groupe GES
item.description: EIML Paris, l’École Internationale de Marketing du Luxe, fait l’objet d’une revendication de fuite de données publiée le... L’article EIML Paris : près de 90 000 personnes exposées après la cyberattaque du Groupe GES…

item.title: [Élevé] École Supérieure des Métiers de l’Immobilier : des dizaines de milliers de dossiers étudiants exposés après une cyberattaque
item.description: L’École Supérieure des Métiers de l’Immobilier fait l’objet d’une revendication de fuite de données publiée le 11 juillet... L’article École Supérieure des Métiers de l’Immobilier : des dizaines de milliers de dossiers…

item.title: [Faible] Lidl : les coordonnées de clients exposés après une cyberattaque chez un prestataire
item.description: Lidl informe une partie de ses clients d’une violation de données survenue chez l’un de ses prestataires informatiques.... L’article Lidl : les coordonnées de clients exposés après une cyberattaque chez un prestataire…

item.title: [Élevé] CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems.
Unlike other information stealers that are built on…

item.title: [Faible] Google and Microsoft Pull ModHeader With 1.6 Million Installs After Dormant Collector Found
item.description: Google and Microsoft have pulled ModHeader, a popular header-editing extension with roughly 1.6 million installs across Chrome and Edge, after researchers found a hidden browsing-history collector built into its…

item.title: [Faible] Russian celebrity journalist Ksenia Sobchak says hackers accessed Telegram channels via email breach
item.description: Following the breach of several of her Telegram channels, controversial Russian journalist Ksenia Sobchak claimed published screenshots of her correspondence with political figures were fake.

item.title: [Critique] CISA warns of actively exploited RCE flaws in Joomla extensions
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the iCagenda and Balbooa Forms extensions for Joomla to achieve remote code execution through…

item.title: [Critique] ⚡ Weekly Recap: ShareFile Threat, Citrix Bleed 2 Ransomware, AI Coding Attacks, and More
item.description: Somewhere right now, a security tool is quietly finding bugs faster than any human can fix them. That's supposed to be the good news. The catch is that the attackers have the same tools, pointed the other way, and they…

item.title: [Faible] Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub…

item.title: [Faible] Lidl discloses online shop breach after service provider hack
item.description: German discount supermarket chain Lidl notified customers in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands that attackers stole their personal information in a breach at a service provider. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Breach at the Beach: Play the Ultimate Entra ID CTF
item.description: Learn how attackers abuse Entra ID through a free hands-on Capture the Flag. Varonis created the Breach at the Beach CTF to teach defenders how to investigate Entra ID attack techniques using realistic scenarios. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New MemGhost Attack Plants Persistent False Memories in AI Agents Through One Email
item.description: Give an AI assistant a memory and access to your inbox, and you hand an attacker a way to rewrite what it thinks it knows about you. A single email can trick that agent into saving a false "fact" about the user, hide…

item.title: [Faible] UK charges suspects linked to Russian Coms call spoofing platform
item.description: UK authorities charged five people following a National Crime Agency (NCA) investigation into Russian Coms, a major caller ID spoofing platform used by criminals to make over 1.8 million scam calls. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted lure creation,…

item.title: [Faible] UK and Allies urge critical sectors to improve defences against Russian intelligence targeting
item.description: New advisory highlights Russian state cyber actors’ global exploitation of poorly configured routers

item.title: [Faible] Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
item.description:
Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every read.
Each read gets…
